description: This figure presents a 3D stacked bar chart illustrating the relative cation fraction (at.%) of different valence states of metallic elements (Al³⁺, Fe²⁺, Fe³⁺ₒₓ, Fe³⁺ₕᵧ, Mn⁴⁺, Mn²⁺) in the surface oxide films of Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels (LDSs). The quantitative data, derived from XPS peak area calculations using sensitivity factors, compares four samples: S0 (as-rolled), S950 (solution-treated at 950°C), S1050 (1050°C), and S1150 (1150°C). The visualization reveals that the fine-grained S950 sample exhibits the highest Al³⁺ content at 90.17 at.%, indicating a highly enriched aluminum oxide film. In contrast, the S0 sample shows significantly lower Al content (~50 at.%) with elevated contributions from iron and manganese species. As grain size increases with solution treatment temperature (from 8.7 μm in S950 to 32 μm in S1150), the Al³⁺ fraction progressively decreases to approximately 73% in S1150, accompanied by corresponding increases in Fe and Mn oxide contents. This trend demonstrates that grain refinement promotes preferential aluminum dissolution and rapid formation of a dense, protective Al-rich oxide film, whereas coarse-grained structures result in oxide films enriched with less-protective Fe and Mn species, thereby explaining the superior corrosion resistance of the S950 sample.
